message:
It's a tale as old as time: you set a cup near the edge of the table, your cat strolls over, makes deliberate eye contact… and slowly pushes it off. 🫣

So why do they do it? It's not spite — it's instinct. That little paw-swat is the same move cats use in the wild to test whether something is alive, to feel its weight and texture, and to learn how the world works through touch. Objects that wobble, roll, or make a satisfying CLUNK are extra irresistible. And honestly? Sometimes they've simply learned that knocking things over gets a big reaction from YOU.

The fix is usually more play and enrichment — a few good toy sessions, a window perch, or a puzzle feeder can redirect all that curiosity into something less… breakable. 💛

Be honest: what's your cat's favorite thing to knock off the table? 😹🐾
